meta.dataNote: IMPORTANT — VERIFY BEFORE ACTING: The official WGU Transfer Pathway Agreement and BAELED Program Guide PDFs were not available when this data was compiled. Course names, codes, credit values and transfer equivalencies below were reconstructed from publicly documented WGU program information and community-reported pathways, and MUST be verified against your official documents. Course codes vary by catalog year. Edit this file to match your official degree plan.
meta.totalCUsNote: Total competency units are computed from the course list below so all dashboard math stays internally consistent. Confirm the official total (typically ~120 CUs) in your Program Guide and add any missing courses here.

courses.name: Composition: Successful Self-Expression
resources: WGU course chatter threads on r/WGU
courses.notes: Two performance tasks (essays). Commonly satisfied by Sophia English Composition I on the published Teachers College pathway — verify code/name for your catalog year.

courses.name: Introduction to Communication: Connecting with Others
courses.notes: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW — communication equivalencies differ between catalogs. Confirm whether Sophia Public Speaking / Communication at Work or Study.com Communications 101 maps to this course in your agreement.

courses.name: Introduction to Humanities
courses.notes: Community-reported: Sophia Ancient Greek Philosophers (or an Art History course) has satisfied the humanities requirement — verify in your agreement.

courses.name: Survey of United States History
courses.notes: Sophia U.S. History I is a commonly published pathway match — verify.

courses.name: American Politics and the US Constitution
courses.notes: Community-reported: Sophia U.S. Government has satisfied this requirement — verify.

courses.name: Introduction to Geography
courses.notes: Sophia has no geography course. Community-reported: Study.com Geography 101 has been used — verify.

courses.name: Health, Fitness, and Wellness
courses.notes: Community-reported: Study.com Health 101 has been used — verify.

courses.name: Applied Probability and Statistics
courses.notes: Sophia Introduction to Statistics is a commonly published pathway match — verify.

courses.name: Critical Thinking: Reason and Evidence
courses.notes: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW — confirm whether this course is on your degree plan and whether any Sophia/Study.com course satisfies it.

courses.name: Integrated Physical Sciences
courses.notes: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW — physical-science equivalency is inconsistent across reports. Verify before purchasing any course for this slot.

courses.name: Natural Science Lab
courses.notes: Community-reported: a Sophia lab science (e.g. Human Biology + Lab) has satisfied the natural science lab requirement — verify.

courses.name: Mathematics for Elementary Educators I
courses.notes: Content course usually completed at WGU. Course code varies by catalog — check your degree plan.

courses.name: Mathematics for Elementary Educators II
courses.notes: Course code varies by catalog — check your degree plan.

courses.name: Mathematics for Elementary Educators III
courses.notes: Course code varies by catalog — check your degree plan.

courses.name: Educational Foundations
courses.notes: Usually the first professional core course. Quick performance task.

courses.name: The School as a Community of Care
courses.notes: Focus on adolescent development and community context.

courses.name: Educational Psychology and Development of Children and Adolescents
courses.notes: Professional core must generally be completed at WGU — psychology transfer credits rarely apply here. Verify before assuming.

courses.name: Fundamentals of Diverse Learners
courses.notes: Covers differentiation, IEPs/504s, and supports for diverse learners.

courses.name: Managing Engaging Learning Environments
courses.notes: Classroom management theory and practice.

courses.name: Introduction to Curriculum, Instruction, and Assessment
courses.notes: First lesson-planning heavy course. Includes preclinical observation hours in many catalogs — verify.

courses.name: Educational Technology for Teaching and Learning
courses.notes: Technology integration and digital citizenship.

courses.name: Assessing Impact on Student Learning
courses.notes: Assessment design, data analysis, and feedback loops.

courses.name: Children's Literature
courses.notes: Course code varies by catalog — check your degree plan.

courses.name: Elementary Reading Methods and Interventions
courses.notes: Heavily aligned with the science of reading. Plan real study time.

courses.name: Language Arts Instruction and Intervention
courses.notes: Course code varies by catalog — check your degree plan.

courses.name: Elementary Mathematics Methods
courses.notes: Lesson planning for elementary math. Code varies by catalog.

courses.name: Elementary Science Methods
courses.notes: Code varies by catalog — check your degree plan.

courses.name: Elementary Social Studies Methods
courses.notes: Code varies by catalog — check your degree plan.

courses.name: Elementary Visual and Performing Arts Methods
courses.notes: Code varies by catalog — check your degree plan.

courses.name: Elementary Physical Education and Health Methods
courses.notes: Code varies by catalog — check your degree plan.

courses.name: Preclinical Experiences in Elementary Education
courses.notes: Requires in-classroom observation hours — start placement paperwork EARLY. Hours requirement varies by state (Virginia licensure rules apply).

courses.name: Supervised Demonstration Teaching I
courses.notes: Full-time student teaching. Must align with the host school's academic calendar.

courses.name: Supervised Demonstration Teaching II
courses.notes: Second half of the student teaching placement.

courses.name: Teacher Performance Assessment in Elementary Education
courses.notes: Capstone-style performance assessment completed during student teaching.

courses.name: Professional Portfolio
courses.notes: Professional portfolio assembled from demonstration teaching artifacts.

courses.name: Cohort Seminar
courses.notes: Runs alongside demonstration teaching.
